zh.lifeSummary: 當William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan於1163年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵時，他的父親Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale48歲，母親Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl41歲。他於1190年與Sarah FitzHugh在英國蘇格蘭希倫德巴登結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1205年被列為justiciar of scotia。他去世於1233年享年70歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵。

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan was born in 1163, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, his father, Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale, was 48 and his mother, Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, was 41. He married Sarah FitzHugh in 1190, in Badenoch, Highland, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. In 1205, his occupation is listed as justiciar of scotia. He died in 1233, at the age of 70, and was buried in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

Il a épousé Sarah FitzHugh en 1190, à Badenoch, Highland, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. En 1205, sa profession de son indiquée est justiciar of scotia. Il est décédé en 1233, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse.

Er hat 1190 in Badenoch, Highland, Schottland, United Kingdom Sarah FitzHugh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1205 ist sein Beruf als justiciar of scotia aufgeführt. Er starb 1233 im Alter von 70 Jahren und wurde in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland begraben.

Lui sposato Sarah FitzHugh nel 1190, nel Badenoch, Highland, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1205, la sua occupazione è indicata come justiciar of scotia. Morì nel 1233, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchanが1163年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンで生まれた時，父Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedaleは48歳，母Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Athollは41歳であった。1190年にイギリススコットランドヒグhランドバデノチでSarah FitzHughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1205年，職業はjusticiar of scotiaと記録されている。彼は1233年に70歳で死去し，スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan(이)가 1163년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1190년 영국 스코틀랜드 히랜드 바데노치에서 Sarah FitzHugh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1205년, 그의 직업은 justiciar of scotia이었다. 그는 1233년 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan nasceu em 1163, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, seu pai, Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah FitzHugh em 1190, em Badenoch, Highland, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1205, sua profissão aparece como justiciar of scotia. Ele faleceu em 1233, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan родился в 1163 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah FitzHugh в 1190 г. в местности баденох, хаиланд, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1205 году он работал по профессии justiciar of scotia. Он умер в 1233 г. в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan nació en 1163, en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, su papá, Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Sarah FitzHugh en 1190, en Badenoch, Highland, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1205, su ocupación registrada era justiciar of scotia. Murió en 1233, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.
